userStories.title: Scaffold browser app
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a minimal browser app scaffold so that the project can render a full-screen experience.
acceptanceCriteria: App starts locally with a single command
A root canvas container is rendered
Production build succeeds
No runtime errors on first load
userStories.notes: Use Vite. Keep the initial scaffold minimal.

userStories.title: Create full-screen canvas shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want the experience to occupy the full viewport so that it feels immersive immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas fills the viewport
Viewport resize updates render size
No scrollbars appear
Canvas is shown on a dark background
userStories.notes: Rendering backend can still be a placeholder.

userStories.title: Read low-entropy browser signals
userStories.description: As a system, I want to read basic browser and device signals so that the scene can be influenced by the current visitor context.
acceptanceCriteria: Read language
Read timezone
Read screen dimensions
Read devicePixelRatio if available
All values stay in memory only
userStories.notes: Do not persist or transmit values.

userStories.title: Read capability and preference signals
userStories.description: As a system, I want to read capability and preference hints so that the scene can adapt to the visitor environment.
acceptanceCriteria: Read hardwareConcurrency if available
Read prefers-color-scheme
Read prefers-reduced-motion
Read touch capability from available browser APIs
All values stay in memory only
userStories.notes: Treat all values as optional.

userStories.title: Expose coarse geo via server endpoint
userStories.description: As a system, I want to expose coarse geolocation hints via a server endpoint so that the client can safely use them without accessing request headers directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Country header is read from Vercel environment
Region header is read from Vercel environment
Geo data is exposed via /api/geo endpoint
Endpoint returns only coarse values (country, region)
Endpoint uses cache-control: no-store
Client does not access request headers directly
App works if geo data is unavailable
userStories.notes: Implement as Vercel serverless/edge function using geolocation().

userStories.title: Track runtime pointer entropy
userStories.description: As a system, I want to collect pointer movement during the session so that the scene can evolve with live visitor interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Pointer position is tracked
Pointer velocity or delta is derived
Mouse and touch both work
No pointer history is persisted
userStories.notes: Normalize coordinates to viewport space.

userStories.title: Generate ephemeral session seed
userStories.description: As a system, I want to combine visitor signals and current time into a session seed so that each visit generates a distinct scene.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed includes current time component
Seed changes on reload
Seed remains stable during one session
Seed is derived without storing source data
userStories.notes: Use a simple deterministic hash.

userStories.title: Define partially legible mapping rules
userStories.description: As a designer, I want explicit mapping rules from visitor signals to visual parameters so that the scene feels personal without revealing raw data.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 4 signal-to-visual mappings are defined
Mappings are indirect rather than literal
Mappings avoid displaying raw identifiers
Mapping rules are documented in code comments or a small config module
userStories.notes: Example: region family to palette temperature, DPR to particle density band, time zone to motion cadence, reduced-motion to calmer motion profile.

userStories.title: Render seeded base scene
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a generated scene based on the session seed so that the work feels unique from the first frame.
acceptanceCriteria: Scene parameters derive from seed
At least one rendered geometry system exists
Scene animates continuously
Scene renders without audio
userStories.notes: Start with one lightweight system such as points or lines.

userStories.title: Map coarse location to palette
userStories.description: As a user, I want geography to subtly influence the visual palette so that the scene feels partially legible.
acceptanceCriteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
Fallback palette is used when geo hints are unavailable
Raw geo values are not displayed in the scene
Palette changes are deterministic within the session
userStories.notes: Use coarse classes instead of exact location labels.

userStories.title: Map device profile to structure
userStories.description: As a user, I want device and browser hints to influence scene structure so that the scene feels linked to the environment I arrived with.
acceptanceCriteria: At least two structural parameters are driven by signals
No raw browser strings are shown
Mapping remains stable within the session
Scene still works if some signals are missing
userStories.notes: Example: touch capability alters curve softness, DPR alters density range.

userStories.title: Add time-based evolution
userStories.description: As a user, I want the scene to change over runtime so that the artwork feels fleeting rather than static.
acceptanceCriteria: Elapsed time changes at least two visual parameters
Scene does not hard reset during runtime
Temporal behavior differs between sessions
Motion remains smooth
userStories.notes: Use seeded modulation curves.

userStories.title: Add pointer influence to scene motion
userStories.description: As a user, I want my pointer movement to alter the scene so that I can perturb the artwork without controlling it directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Pointer changes at least one motion parameter
Effect decays when pointer becomes idle
Touch and mouse both influence the scene
Idle mode still looks intentional
userStories.notes: Treat interaction as entropy, not direct manipulation.

userStories.title: Load audio files from public folder
userStories.description: As a system, I want to load licensed audio tracks from a public folder so that the site can play background music without external services.
acceptanceCriteria: Tracks are loaded from /public/audio
At least one supported audio format is playable
Missing or invalid files fail gracefully
No external streaming service is required
userStories.notes: Prefer mp3 or ogg for browser compatibility.

userStories.title: Randomize track selection
userStories.description: As a user, I want tracks to be selected randomly so that repeated visits do not begin identically.
acceptanceCriteria: A track is chosen randomly at start
Next track is chosen randomly after track end
System handles single-track and multi-track folders
Selection does not require persistent memory
userStories.notes: Avoid immediate repeat when more than one track exists.

userStories.title: Attempt muted autoplay
userStories.description: As a user, I want the audio system to begin in muted mode automatically when allowed so that the visualizer can start without friction.
acceptanceCriteria: Playback is attempted in muted mode
Autoplay failure is handled without crashing
Visuals continue even if autoplay is blocked
Audio can later be enabled by user action
userStories.notes: Respect browser autoplay rules.

userStories.title: Create analyser pipeline
userStories.description: As a system, I want real-time audio analysis so that the scene can react to the music.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio source is connected to an AnalyserNode
Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
Mute state does not disable analysis
userStories.notes: Keep the audio graph simple.

userStories.title: Map bass response to macro motion
userStories.description: As a user, I want low frequencies to influence large-scale movement so that the audiovisual connection feels perceptible.
acceptanceCriteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
Response is smoothed to avoid jitter
Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted
No clipping or visual instability occurs
userStories.notes: Examples: camera drift, field expansion, large wave amplitude.

userStories.title: Map treble response to fine detail
userStories.description: As a user, I want high frequencies to influence detail behavior so that the scene reflects the texture of the audio.
acceptanceCriteria: Treble band influences at least one detail property
Response is smoothed to avoid flicker
Effect is visibly distinct from bass mapping
Scene remains readable
userStories.notes: Examples: sparkle, point jitter, line brightness modulation.

userStories.title: Add mute and unmute control
userStories.description: As a user, I want a mute control so that I can choose whether to hear the soundtrack.
acceptanceCriteria: A visible control toggles audio gain
Toggle updates instantly
Control is accessible while the scene is running
Audio analysis continues while muted
userStories.notes: Use a GainNode rather than tearing down playback.

userStories.title: Add info button shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want an info button so that I can discover what the work is about.
acceptanceCriteria: Info button is visible
Button opens an overlay
Overlay closes without reload
Overlay works on desktop and mobile
userStories.notes: Keep UI minimal and unobtrusive.

userStories.title: Write privacy and artwork copy
userStories.description: As a user, I want a short explanation of the artwork and its privacy model so that I understand the intent.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay states this is an art installation
Overlay states no data is stored
Overlay states visitor context influences the scene
Copy stays concise
userStories.notes: Avoid legal overstatement. Keep language simple.

userStories.title: Show partially legible influence hints
userStories.description: As a user, I want subtle examples of what influences the scene so that the mapping feels discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay names categories of influence such as time, device, location, and motion
Overlay does not reveal raw identifier values
Hints align with actual implemented mappings
Hints can be updated from a single source of truth
userStories.notes: Keep this conceptual rather than forensic.

userStories.title: Add reduced-motion fallback
userStories.description: As a user with reduced-motion preference, I want calmer movement so that the work remains comfortable to view.
acceptanceCriteria: Reduced-motion preference is detected
Motion amplitude is lowered
Core visual identity is preserved
No separate page is required
userStories.notes: Do not disable the work entirely.

userStories.title: Add mobile quality scaling
userStories.description: As a user on a mobile device, I want the scene to degrade gracefully so that the site remains usable.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity
Touch input remains supported
Mobile browsers do not crash on load
Visual output stays recognizably similar
userStories.notes: Scale particle counts, shader complexity, or post-processing.

userStories.title: Create second visual mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want occasional structural variation so that longer sessions feel less repetitive.
acceptanceCriteria: A second mode is implemented
Both modes reuse the same seed and audio inputs
Mode choice is based on seed or elapsed time
Mode switch does not break playback
userStories.notes: Keep mode boundaries clean so more can be added later.

userStories.title: Add runtime health check
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a lightweight validation pass so that tickets are only closed when the experience runs cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Production build succeeds
No console errors on normal load
Scene renders when audio is blocked
Mute toggle and info overlay both work
userStories.notes: Use this as a recurring definition-of-done gate.
